metadata.name: Encore Briarblade Adept — exile from graveyard, create 3 tokens in 4-player game
metadata.description: In a 4-player Commander game, p1 has Briarblade Adept ({4}{B}, 3/4 Elf Assassin, Encore {3}{B}) in the graveyard with {3}{B} mana available. p1 activates encore on Briarblade Adept (paying {3}{B} and exiling the card as cost per CR 702.141a and CR 602.2c). The card is immediately exiled before the ability is placed on the stack. All four players pass priority. The encore ability resolves, creating one token copy of Briarblade Adept per opponent — three tokens total (one per p2, p3, p4) — each with Haste and flagged for sacrifice at the next end step. Final assert: original Briarblade Adept is in exile, three tokens with is_token=true are on p1's battlefield, stack is empty.
metadata.generation_notes: Briarblade Adept card definition confirmed in crates/engine/src/cards/defs/briarblade_adept.rs: {4}{B}, Creature — Elf Assassin 3/4, AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Encore) + AbilityDefinition::Encore { cost: ManaCost { generic: 3, black: 1 } }. The attack trigger for -1/-1 is omitted from the card definition due to the targeted_trigger DSL gap (W5 worklist). CR 702.141a: 'Encore [cost]' means '[Cost], Exile this card from your graveyard: For each opponent, create a token that's a copy of this card that attacks that opponent this turn if able. The tokens gain haste. Sacrifice them at the beginning of the next end step. Activate only as a sorcery.' Encore cost {3}{B}: mana_paid { colorless: 3, black: 1 }. Per CR 602.2c, all activation costs are paid before the ability is placed on the stack — so Briarblade Adept is immediately exiled at activation time (analogous to embalm/eternalize behavior confirmed in scripts 129 and 130). In a 4-player Commander game, p1 has 3 opponents (p2, p3, p4), so 3 tokens are created upon resolution. The 'encore_card' action type is supported in translate_player_action (replay_harness.rs line 591). 'Activate only as a sorcery' satisfied: precombat main phase, active player p1, empty stack. The end-step sacrifice trigger (EncoreSacrificeTrigger) cannot be driven via the script harness because the harness does not advance phases via PassPriority — the engine's turn advancement logic (end_step_actions) is not triggered from script priority_rounds. The sacrifice behavior is already covered by native unit test test_encore_sacrifice_at_end_step in crates/engine/tests/encore.rs. Script scope intentionally limited to activation + resolution. Dispute struct requires: step_index, action_index, raised_by (required String), description, resolution, resolved_by, resolved_date — all optional except raised_by.

disputes.description: End-step sacrifice behavior (EncoreSacrificeTrigger, CR 702.141a) cannot be tested via the script replay harness. The harness issues PassPriority commands but does not trigger the engine's end_step_actions() which queues encore sacrifice triggers at phase entry. This is a harness limitation, not an engine gap. The sacrifice behavior is fully covered by native unit test test_encore_sacrifice_at_end_step in crates/engine/tests/encore.rs. Script scope intentionally limited to activation (exile as cost) + encore ability resolution (3 tokens created).

script.step_note: p1 activates encore on Briarblade Adept — card is immediately exiled as cost (CR 602.2c), encore ability goes on stack, all four players pass priority, ability resolves creating 3 Briarblade Adept tokens (one per opponent)

actions.description: Initial: Briarblade Adept in p1 graveyard, battlefield empty, exile empty, stack empty
actions.note: Briarblade Adept is in the graveyard awaiting encore activation. p1 has {3}{B} (colorless: 3, black: 1) in pool to pay the encore cost. Exile is empty. Stack is empty. 4-player game: p1 has 3 opponents (p2, p3, p4) — encore will create 3 tokens upon resolution per CR 702.141a.

CR 702.141a: p1 activates Encore {3}{B} on Briarblade Adept in the graveyard. Cost includes {3}{B} mana (generic 3 + black 1) and exiling Briarblade Adept from the graveyard. CR 602.2c: The exile cost is paid at activation time — Briarblade Adept immediately moves to exile before the ability is placed on the stack. 'Activate only as a sorcery' is legal: precombat main phase, active player p1, empty stack.

actions.description: After encore activation: Briarblade Adept is in exile (cost paid per CR 602.2c), encore ability on stack (count 1), graveyard empty
actions.note: CR 602.2c: Briarblade Adept was exiled as part of paying the encore activation cost — it is in exile, not the graveyard. The encore activated ability is now on the stack. The ability will create one token per opponent (p2, p3, p4) when it resolves.
CR 116.3: All four players pass priority in succession — encore ability resolves from top of stack.

resolution.note: CR 702.141a: Encore ability resolves — for each opponent (p2, p3, p4), create a token that's a copy of Briarblade Adept. 3 tokens total created on p1's battlefield. Each token is a 3/4 Elf Assassin copy of Briarblade Adept with Haste (gained per CR 702.141a) and encore_sacrifice_at_end_step=true. CR 111.1 + 111.5: tokens enter p1's battlefield.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Encore ability resolves. Three Briarblade Adept tokens (3/4 Elf Assassin with Haste) are created on p1's battlefield — one targeting p2, one targeting p3, one targeting p4. NOTE: stack_resolve is informational only — the engine already processed resolution via the prior priority_round pass.

actions.description: Final: 3 Briarblade Adept tokens on p1 battlefield (is_token=true), original Briarblade Adept in exile, stack empty, all life totals unchanged
actions.note: CR 702.141a: Three 3/4 Elf Assassin tokens (encore copies of Briarblade Adept) are on p1's battlefield — one per opponent (p2, p3, p4). Each has Haste and encore_sacrifice_at_end_step=true. The original Briarblade Adept is in exile (exiled as activation cost, per CR 602.2c). Stack is empty. Life totals unchanged. The end-step sacrifice (EncoreSacrificeTrigger) is not testable via the script harness; it is covered by native unit test test_encore_sacrifice_at_end_step in encore.rs.
